workflow.name: fr_copyedit_conservative
workflow.purpose: French academic copyedit with strict meaning preservation. Do NOT propose rephrasing unless a sentence is truly too obscure.

non_negotiables.item: Never apply silent edits. All edits must be suggestions only (track changes).
Anchor comments to the smallest relevant span possible.
If uncertain, prefer a comment over an edit.

tasks.item: Correct errors in all forms: spelling, conjugation, typos, missing words inferred from meaning, missing conjunctions, and tense agreement issues within a paragraph.
Identify repetitions and suggest alternatives/synonyms that do not create a new repetition with another nearby word in the text.
Check French agreement with respect to the German word: agree French terms according to the grammatical gender of the German word (e.g., “Mittelgebirge”). If unsure, add a comment instead of editing.

List words/phrases that can be removed without changing the meaning. Often overused adverbs (e.g., “en particulier”, “notamment”) when they add no real nuance; also redundant phrases (e.g., “de l’empire” in the provided example).
Ensure consistency in typographic standards within the chapter.
Check footnotes. Verify online that cited bibliographic references are correct when feasible; otherwise, comment what to verify.
Verify numerical consistency (internal contradictions or mismatched figures).
Flag if a footnote contains no reference or indicates that a reference must be added.
Correct German terms and other foreign-language terms, including inside bibliographic references.

Check punctuation; consistent uppercase/lowercase (especially plant names: harmonize across the document by searching similar occurrences); harmonize alternative spellings (e.g., “chaînes de commodités” vs “chaînes de commodité”).
Flag inconsistencies in cross-references to figures.
List archival call numbers cited in the chapter in a single paragraph (to facilitate the “sources” section of the bibliography).

workflow.rephrasing_policy: Do not propose rewording except when a sentence is truly too obscure. In that case, propose the smallest possible rewrite that preserves meaning.
